title: trichomanes griffithii
intro: Trichomanes griffithii, also known as the Griffith's fork moss, is a rare and endangered species of liverwort, a small non-vascular plant that belongs to the division Marchantiophyta. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Trichomanes griffithii include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Trichomanes griffithii is typically prepared as a decoction or a tincture, with the plant being harvested from the wild and dried for later use in herbal preparations.
